commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] This needs human product/security review for Windows scope and signing; the code defects are narrow but not the only merge blocker.; Needs attention: The current diff adds security-sensitive Windows token storage, OAuth, release signing, and remote script execution; no current secrets are reported, but maintainer security review is still needed.; Review finding: [P2] Keep logger tests out of real AppData logs... (sha=defd948d43c4f10a75afcebf7650e60618e01bbf)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a native .NET/WinForms Windows taskbar tray companion with Windows build/package/smoke scripts, CI/signing workflow, documentation, screenshot proof, and xUnit coverage.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a new-platform feature PR, not a bug report. Source inspection confirms current main has no Windows source tree while the PR adds one.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] This needs human product/security review for Windows scope and signing; the code defects are narrow but not the only merge blocker.

target.title: feat(windows): add taskbar tray companion
target.body:
## Summary
- Add a native Windows taskbar tray companion under `Windows/` with repository status rows, local Git state, recent GitHub lists, global Commits and Activity feeds, Actions queue/billing/cache/artifact-retention/runner details, rate-limit diagnostics, PR notifications, Issue Navigator, clipboard reference monitoring, Preferences, named accounts, Credential Manager token storage, tray logout, ETag cache reset, archive fallback, launch-at-login, update checks, installer packaging, and Windows CI.
- Keep moving the Windows Issue Navigator toward macOS parity: source-host GitHub URLs, Actions run URLs, commit URLs and hashes, direct issue inputs, unique repo-name shorthands, chained/ranged references, plural prose lists, pasted-order preservation, and clipboard balloon handoff.
- Add Windows build/package/smoke/Crabbox scripts with TRX/smoke artifacts and desktop screenshot proof.
- Document Windows setup, current parity surface, screenshot proof, and trusted CI code signing using the same Azure Artifact Signing account/profile as the OpenClaw Windows node app.

## Screenshots
Windows tray/menu screenshot checked into this branch at `docs/assets/repobar-windows-tray-menu.png`:

![RepoBar Windows tray menu screenshot](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vincentkoc/RepoBar/5e321be48eb8f2766e0307522c5d3c4dd5e90919/docs/assets/repobar-windows-tray-menu.png)

Crabbox VNC desktop capture from the interactive Windows proof run:

![RepoBar Windows Crabbox VNC tray menu screenshot](https://gist.githubusercontent.com/vincentkoc/ec4abf082bdf3c280f0c940e36e1be8b/raw/3806aeabe7d6b9c49b543260067b48f4229cb24b/repobar-windows-tray-menu.png)

Additional Crabbox taskbar proof card: https://gist.githubusercontent.com/vincentkoc/ec4abf082bdf3c280f0c940e36e1be8b/raw/ac1a574c07e5e6425c12010537140851b855b31a/repobar-windows-current-head-taskbar.svg

## Validation
- Commit `5e321be`: moved Windows proof-artifact validation after x64 and ARM64 packaging, made `Test-WindowsValidationArtifacts.ps1` require both `win-x64` and `win-arm64` publish layouts with `RepoBar.Windows.exe`, and uploaded the compact `repobar-windows-validation` manifest separately for PR review. Static `git diff --check`, cached whitespace check, Ruby YAML parsing, and `package.json` JSON parsing passed. GitGuardian Security Checks passed on the pushed head. the targeted `dotnet test --filter WindowsReleaseSigningWorkflowTests` could not start because local `dotnet` is unavailable. Direct Crabbox `whoami` still returns `401 unauthorized`, so hosted Windows validation remains environment-blocked. Upstream GitHub Actions for fork PRs remains `action_required` pending maintainer approval.
- Commit `5a24ec1`: exposed the Windows proof/package helpers through pnpm with `windows:package:arm64` and `windows:validate-artifacts`, and documented the wrappers in README plus `docs/windows.md`. `package.json` parses as JSON, static `git diff --check`, and cached whitespace checks passed. GitGuardian Security Checks passed on the pushed head. Attempting `pnpm windows:validate-artifacts` did not reach the script because Corepack failed before pnpm install with local disk `ENOSPC`; local `dotnet`/`pwsh` remain unavailable. Direct Crabbox `whoami` still returns `401 unauthorized`, so hosted Windows validation remains environment-blocked. Upstream GitHub Actions for fork PRs remains `action_required` pending maintainer approval.
- Commit `defd948`: added CI coverage for the documented `win-arm64` Windows layout by packaging `Scripts/package_windows.ps1 -Runtime win-arm64 -SkipInstaller` and uploading `repobar-windows-win-arm64-unsigned`, while leaving the trusted signing/installer path explicitly scoped to the existing `win-x64` layout. Updated docs, parity notes, changelog, and workflow regression coverage. Static `git diff --check`, cached whitespace check, and Ruby YAML parsing passed; GitGuardian Security Checks passed on the pushed head. the targeted `dotnet test --filter WindowsReleaseSigningWorkflowTests` could not start because local `dotnet` is unavailable. Direct Crabbox `whoami` still returns `401 unauthorized`, so hosted Windows validation remains environment-blocked. Upstream GitHub Actions for fork PRs remains `action_required` pending maintainer approval.
- Commit `98a517c`: added `Scripts/Test-WindowsValidationArtifacts.ps1` and wired CI to validate Windows proof artifacts after the launch smoke. The validator checks the TRX counters, required smoke proof booleans, local Git/archive fallback evidence, rendered menu proof, and writes `repobar-windows-validation.json` into the smoke artifact directory. Updated docs/parity notes and workflow regression coverage for the validator step. Static `git diff --check`, cached whitespace check, and Ruby YAML parsing passed; GitGuardian Security Checks passed on the pushed head. the targeted `dotnet test --filter WindowsReleaseSigningWorkflowTests` could not start because local `dotnet` is unavailable. Direct Crabbox `whoami` still returns `401 unauthorized`, so hosted Windows validation remains environment-blocked. Upstream GitHub Actions for fork PRs remains `action_required` pending maintainer approval.
- Commit `01ec58b`: added Windows workflow regression coverage that locks the CI launch-smoke and artifact contract: `repobar-windows-test-results`, `repobar-windows-smoke`, the smoke command, warning behavior for missing artifacts, and ordering before packaging. Static `git diff --check` and Ruby YAML parsing passed; GitGuardian Security Checks passed on the pushed head. the targeted `dotnet test --filter WindowsReleaseSigningWorkflowTests` could not start because local `dotnet` is unavailable. Direct Crabbox `whoami` still returns `401 unauthorized`, so hosted Windows validation remains environment-blocked. Upstream GitHub Actions for fork PRs remains `action_required` pending maintainer approval.
- Commit `3fb826d`: wired the Windows CI job to upload `dist/windows/test-results` as `repobar-windows-test-results`, run `Scripts/smoke_windows.ps1 -Runtime win-x64 -LaunchSeconds 5`, and upload `dist/windows/smoke` as `repobar-windows-smoke` so approved fork/main CI captures TRX plus runtime launch-smoke JSON/screenshot artifacts. Updated the Windows docs and parity contract with those artifact names. Static `git diff --check` and Ruby YAML parsing passed; GitGuardian Security Checks passed on the pushed head. local `actionlint`, `dotnet`, `pwsh`, and `powershell` are unavailable, and disk remains effectively full. Direct Crabbox `whoami` still returns `401 unauthorized`, so hosted Windows validation remains environment-blocked. Upstream GitHub Actions for fork PRs remains `action_required` pending maintainer approval.
- Commit `1c9e76e`: expanded the Windows launch smoke so the running tray process writes refresh/update, repository scope/sort/display, local scan/fetch/sync/dirty-file, Actions, clipboard reference monitor, and PR notification settings into the runtime JSON artifact. The PowerShell smoke now fails if those observed runtime settings do not match the smoke configuration, and the final proof line includes those booleans for PR review. Static `git diff --check` passed; GitGuardian Security Checks passed on the pushed head. local `dotnet`, `pwsh`, and `powershell` are unavailable, and disk remains effectively full after `agent-worktree-maintain --force`. Direct Crabbox `whoami` still returns `401 unauthorized`, so hosted Windows validation remains environment-blocked. Upstream GitHub Actions for fork PRs remains `action_required` pending maintainer approval.
- Commit `e80f9f3`: expanded the copyable Windows diagnostics proof to include refresh/update settings, repository scope/sort/display limit, local project scan/fetch/sync/dirty-file settings, Actions monitored owners, clipboard reference monitoring, and PR notification click behavior. Added regression coverage for the copied diagnostics text and human summary, and documented the expanded proof surface. Static `git diff --check` passed; local `dotnet` and `pwsh` are unavailable. GitGuardian Security Checks passed on the pushed head. Direct Crabbox `whoami` still returns `401 unauthorized`, so hosted Windows validation remains environment-blocked. Upstream GitHub Actions for fork PRs remains `action_required` pending maintainer approval.
- Commit `649b10f`: threaded the refreshed Windows local Git index into clipboard reference monitoring and Issue Navigator so copied terminal snippets containing an exact discovered local project path resolve bare refs against that local repository before falling back to the selected repository. Local discovered repositories also participate in repo-name shorthand for the active GitHub host, while host-mismatched local repos are ignored. Added Windows clipboard monitor regression coverage and documented the behavior. Static `git diff --check` passed; local `dotnet`/`pwsh`/`powershell` are unavailable. Direct Crabbox `whoami` still returns `401 unauthorized`, so hosted Windows validation remains environment-blocked. Upstream GitHub Actions for fork PRs remains `action_required` pending maintainer approval.
- Commit `7c4c535`: added single repository-only list item context parity so copied selections like `1. openclaw/Peekaboo` scope later bare refs such as `PR #139` and `#138` to that repository, while multiple repository list items remain default-scoped and consumed repo-heading blocks do not leak unrelated context. Normal prose context like `Found in openclaw/gogcli` still wins over the list-item fallback. Added Windows regression coverage matching the macOS fixtures and documented the Issue Navigator surface. Static `git diff --check` passed; local `dotnet`/`pwsh`/`powershell` are unavailable. Direct Crabbox `whoami` still returns `401 unauthorized`, so hosted Windows validation remains environment-blocked. Upstream GitHub Actions for fork PRs remains `action_required` pending maintainer approval.
- Commit `29263d3`: added leading triage-list priority so pasted lists with multiple leading refs like `- #597` and `- #461/#460` surface those primary refs while suppressing incidental item-detail refs such as `Foundation for #596`, `PR #462 closed`, and indented follow-up duplicates. The same primary refs inherit normal prose repository context when present. Added Windows regression coverage matching the macOS fixture behavior and documented the Issue Navigator surface. Static `git diff --check` passed; local `dotnet`/`pwsh`/`powershell` are unavailable. Direct Crabbox `whoami` still returns `401 unauthorized`, so hosted Windows validation remains environment-blocked. Upstream GitHub Actions for fork PRs remains `action_required` pending maintainer approval.
- Commit `652026a`: added normal prose repository context parity so copied triage text like `Found in openclaw/gogcli` scopes later bare list refs to that repository, while repository-heading child prose does not leak outside its indented block and explicit `other/repo: PR 18` lines still win over ambient context. Added regression coverage matching the macOS fixtures and documented the behavior. Static `git diff --check` passed; local `dotnet`/`pwsh`/`powershell` are unavailable. Crabbox `whoami` still returns `401 unauthorized`; the required `pnpm windows:crabbox` rerun could not reach Crabbox because Corepack attempted to cache pnpm and failed with `ENOSPC` on this 100% full local disk. Upstream GitHub Actions for fork PRs remains `action_required` pending maintainer approval.
- Commit `2c9852e`: added primary URL list shortcut parity with macOS so numbered items like `1. #2172` followed by an indented GitHub URL for `2172` resolve only to the URL-scoped repository, while incidental same-item refs such as `linked #874` and same-number prose like `Later b/repo#10` stay suppressed. Added regression coverage around repository-heading blocks and split lists. Static `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD` passed; local `dotnet`/`pwsh`/`powershell` are unavailable. The required Crabbox Windows rerun was attempted on this head but Crabbox `whoami` and broker lease creation returned `401 unauthorized`; upstream GitHub Actions for fork PRs remains `action_required` pending maintainer approval.
- Commit `619dbee`: extended explicit repository parsing to match the macOS line-scoped fixtures for mixed issue/PR series (`other/repo issue #7 and PR #8`), repeated explicit kinds (`other/repo PR #7 and PR #8`), repository-scoped `gh-42`, and punctuation boundaries that return later refs to the selected repository. Added regression coverage and documented the Issue Navigator behavior. Static `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD` passed; local `dotnet`/`pwsh`/`powershell` are unavailable. The required Crabbox Windows rerun was attempted on this head but Crabbox `whoami` and broker lease creation returned `401 unauthorized`; upstream GitHub Actions for fork PRs remains `action_required` pending maintainer approval.
- Commit `5971523`: switched RepoBar Windows signing CI to the same Azure Trusted Signing lane used by the OpenClaw Windows node app: `azure/trusted-signing-action@v2`, `https://wus2.codesigning.azure.net/`, signing account `hanselman`, certificate profile `WindowsEdgeLight`, and `AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET` credentials. Updated the release-signing guard tests and Windows docs. Static `git diff --check HEAD~2..HEAD` and YAML parsing passed; `actionlint` is not installed locally. The required Crabbox Windows rerun was attempted on this head but Crabbox `whoami` and broker lease creation returned `401 unauthorized`; upstream GitHub Actions for fork PRs remains `action_required` pending maintainer approval.
- Commit `1750384`: line-bounded explicit repository reference series so pasted refs like `other/repo PR #7` do not consume `and #8` from the next line, while same-line series and repository-heading sentence-boundary cases keep macOS parity. Added regression coverage from the macOS fixture set and documented the behavior. Static `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD` passed before the signing follow-up; local `dotnet`/`pwsh`/`powershell` are unavailable. The required Crabbox Windows rerun was attempted on that head but Crabbox `whoami` and broker lease creation returned `401 unauthorized`.
- Commit `948835f`: made heading-row parsing claim explicit repository spans before generic heading-scoped parsing, so lines like `#18 depends on other/repo #7` and `#18 depends on other/repo #18` preserve both the heading repository reference and the explicit repository reference without duplicate default/heading-scoped refs. Added regression coverage for leading heading refs before explicit refs and same-number cross-repository refs. Static `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD` passed; local `dotnet`/`pwsh`/`powershell` are unavailable. The required Crabbox Windows rerun was attempted on this head but Crabbox `whoami` and broker lease creation returned `401 unauthorized`; upstream GitHub Actions for fork PRs remains `action_required` pending maintainer approval.
- Commit `03d0e6a`: made same-line repository prose references such as `openclaw/Tachikoma: PR 18` resolve against the explicit repository, while keeping count-summary tails like `openclaw/Tachikoma: 1 issue / 1 PR 18` and nonleading prose like `openclaw/Tachikoma: issue PR 18` on the normal/default parser path. Added regression coverage matching the macOS translator fixtures and documented the parity behavior. Static `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD` passed; local `dotnet`/`pwsh`/`powershell` are unavailable. The required Crabbox Windows rerun was attempted on this head but Crabbox `whoami` and broker lease creation returned `401 unauthorized`; upstream GitHub Actions for fork PRs remains `action_required` pending maintainer approval.
- Commit `0ec7787`: made explicit repository prose series such as `other/repo PR 7 and 8` and `other/repo issues #8 and #9` resolve against the explicit repository instead of dropping or default-scoping continuation numbers inside heading rows. Added regression coverage matching the macOS translator fixtures and documented the parity behavior. Static `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD` passed; local `dotnet`/`pwsh`/`powershell` are unavailable. The required Crabbox Windows rerun was attempted on this head but Crabbox `whoami` and broker lease creation returned `401 unauthorized`; upstream GitHub Actions for fork PRs remains `action_required` pending maintainer approval.
- Commit `fc158c6`: made the Windows Issue Navigator resolve numbered repository-only headings followed by nested count summaries, such as `1. owner/repo` then `- 0 issues / 1 PR` then `- PR #44`, while keeping sibling repository contexts separate and clearing stale pending headings when a normal repo count heading appears. Added regression coverage matching the macOS translator fixtures and documented the parity behavior. Static `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD` passed; local `dotnet`/`pwsh`/`powershell` are unavailable. The required Crabbox Windows rerun was attempted on this head but Crabbox `whoami` and broker lease creation returned `401 unauthorized`; upstream GitHub Actions for fork PRs remains `action_required` pending maintainer approval.
- Commit `11acfc2`: made repository-heading child rows carry issue/PR reference context to pronoun-led follow-up lines such as `PR #18.` followed by `This is 19.`, while refusing to arm that carryover from trailing non-reference sentences or count summaries like `Open issues: 1`. Added regression coverage matching the macOS translator fixtures and documented the parity behavior. Static `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD` passed; local `dotnet`/`pwsh`/`powershell` are unavailable. The required Crabbox Windows rerun was attempted on this head but Crabbox `whoami` and broker lease creation returned `401 unauthorized`; upstream GitHub Actions for fork PRs remains `action_required` pending maintainer approval.
- Commit `69bdfc1`: made heading-child prose references such as `#18 also fixes PR 19 and 20` stay scoped to the active repository heading instead of falling back to the selected default repository. Added regression coverage and documented the parity behavior. Static `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD` passed; local `dotnet`/`pwsh`/`powershell` are unavailable. The required Crabbox Windows rerun was attempted on this head but Crabbox `whoami` and broker lease creation returned `401 unauthorized`; upstream GitHub Actions for fork PRs remains `action_required` pending maintainer approval.
- Commit `eef6b53`: made the Windows Issue Navigator and clipboard reference parser resolve heading-scoped compound bare shorthands such as `#61/#62` and `#64-#65` against the repository heading, matching the macOS translator behavior for nested repo-summary lists. Added regression coverage and documented the parity behavior. Static `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD` passed; local `dotnet`/`pwsh`/`powershell` are unavailable. The required Crabbox Windows rerun was attempted on this head but Crabbox `whoami` and broker lease creation returned `401 unauthorized`; upstream GitHub Actions for fork PRs remains `action_required` pending maintainer approval.
- Commit `3cd50b3`: made the Windows Issue Navigator and clipboard reference parser resolve commit hashes inside repo-heading child rows, including `commit 4992546` and `commit:` followed by a hash on the next indented line, against the heading repository instead of the selected default repository while still ignoring hex prose without commit context. Added regression coverage and documented the parity behavior. Static `git diff --check` passed; local `dotnet`/`pwsh`/`powershell` are unavailable. The required Crabbox Windows rerun was attempted on this head but Crabbox `whoami` and broker lease creation returned `401 unauthorized`; upstream GitHub Actions for fork PRs remains `action_required` pending maintainer approval.
- Commit `48e973f`: made the Windows Issue Navigator and clipboard reference parser resolve grouped repository issue lists such as `owner/repo: #1, #2` and indented references under repo count headings such as `owner/repo: 1 issue / 1 PR`, while stopping that context at unindented lines. Added regression coverage and documented the parity behavior. Static `git diff --check` passed; local `dotnet`/`pwsh`/`powershell` are unavailable. The required Crabbox Windows rerun was attempted on this head but Crabbox `whoami` and broker lease creation returned `401 unauthorized`; upstream GitHub Actions for fork PRs remains `action_required` pending maintainer approval.
- Commit `efa0910`: made the Windows Issue Navigator and clipboard reference monitor resolve commit URLs, pull-request changes commit URLs, and copied/direct commit hashes against the selected repository, while keeping normal direct issue-number inputs and short hex words distinct. Added regression coverage for source-host preservation, hash URL building, shared-prefix hash dedupe, and clipboard display. Static `git diff --check` passed; local `dotnet`/`pwsh`/`powershell` are unavailable. The required Crabbox Windows rerun was attempted on this head but Crabbox `whoami` and broker lease creation returned `401 unauthorized`; upstream GitHub Actions for fork PRs remains `action_required` pending maintainer approval.
- Commit `9fc5ee9`: made the Windows Issue Navigator resolve direct selected-repository issue inputs such as `73655`, `#42`, `gh-42`, and `GH-42` while leaving incidental prose numbers ignored. Added regression coverage and documentation. Static `git diff --check` passed; Crabbox rerun hit the same `401 unauthorized` blocker.
- Commit `1475cf2`: recognized plural prose reference lists such as `PRs 123 and 456`, `pull requests 123 and 456`, and `issues 12 and 13` while keeping status counters ignored. Static `git diff --check` passed; Crabbox rerun hit the same `401 unauthorized` blocker.
- Commit `f35f852`: recognized GitHub Actions run URLs, preserved source hosts, round-tripped Actions URLs from the preview grid, avoided workflow-run/issue dedupe collisions, and widened run IDs to 64-bit values.
- Commit `26e9bd9`: resolved unique known repo-name shorthands such as `discrawl#64` against the active account repository list and suppressed ambiguous/unknown shorthands.
- Commit `0b243a8`: expanded chained and ranged owner/repo shorthands such as `owner/repo#70/#71`, `owner/repo#66-#69`, and `owner/repo#66-69`, preserving pull-request kind.
- Earlier Crabbox Windows runs validated the implemented tray surface on Windows Server 10.0.20348 with .NET SDK 8.0.421: `dotnet build` succeeded, `dotnet test` passed the then-current Windows test suite, `smoke_windows.ps1` published/launched `RepoBar.Windows.exe`, wrote smoke JSON/TRX artifacts, and verified account profiles, archive fallback, diagnostics, local Git, update diagnostics, and menu proof paths. The interactive Crabbox desktop proof captured the tray/menu screenshot now included above.
- Current hosted proof blocker: Crabbox now returns `401 unauthorized` before provisioning, and GitHub Actions fork PR runs are `action_required`. Attempting to approve the latest run returned `403 Must have admin rights to Repository`.

## Notes
- This stays draft because Windows is end-to-end validated for the implemented surface, but true full macOS feature parity is still broader than the current Windows companion.
- The SSH smoke cannot take an in-process WinForms screenshot on the Crabbox image; durable UI proof uses Crabbox's VNC framebuffer capture, which is the right layer for interactive desktop evidence.
